description: Issue #212 adversarial planted case: a single inserted hunk co-locates the exempt phrase 'hold harmless the other party' (mutual-sounding, order: FIRST) with a separate, non-exempt one-way 'Exos shall indemnify Institution' violation (order: SECOND). Under the old hunk-wide _is_exempted logic, the exempt phrase's presence anywhere in the hunk suppressed ALL trigger fires, including the unrelated one-way indemnity clause -- a detector bypass. Must still fire no-exos-indemnity via SPAN-level exemption: only the 'hold harmless' occurrence that falls inside the exempt phrase's own span is suppressed; the separate 'Exos shall indemnify' occurrence is not.

planted_variation.inserted_hunk: Each party shall hold harmless the other party from ordinary third-party claims arising in the normal course of business. In addition, Exos shall indemnify Institution for any and all claims, damages, and losses, without limit.
planted_variation.notes: Issue #212 repro (order: exempt phrase first, violation second). 'hold harmless the other party' matches exempt_terms verbatim and correctly suppresses the co-located 'hold harmless' trigger_term. The separate 'exos shall indemnify' regex trigger match is a different span entirely and must still fire -- it is not itself inside any exempt-phrase span.

detector_expectation.rationale: Span-level exemption: 'hold harmless the other party' exempts only the trigger match spatially inside it. 'Exos shall indemnify Institution' is a separate, non-exempt span and must fire no-exos-indemnity via the 'exos shall indemnif' regex trigger.
